assertions.title: All pages use api.ts for data fetching

steps.observed: All page files listed (build, connect, domain, index, knowledge, problem, solution, vibe, work).
No matches; grep exited 1 as expected for empty result.

assertions.title: API deduplication — no inline fetch in page scripts

steps.expected: Inline page fetch usage is removed/reduced in favor of shared API helpers
steps.observed: All page files reported count 0 for fetch(.

steps.expected: Interactive Alpine pages import shared API helpers
steps.observed: Alpine pages (build/connect/domain/vibe) import ../lib/api helpers and define Alpine data blocks without inline fetch calls.

assertions.title: Pipeline persistence file is valid YAML

steps.expected: Valid YAML containing step status entries for all 8 pipeline steps

steps.action: Run Ruby YAML parse check (YAML.load_file) and inspect keys/status entries
steps.expected: YAML parses and includes 8 step entries with status fields
steps.observed: YAML parse succeeded, but top-level keys are only completed_steps,current_step_index with 0 status-bearing step entries.

assertions.issues: pipeline_state.yaml is parseable YAML but does not contain explicit status entries for all 8 steps.

frictions.description: Attempting optional precondition check via `mix bro.status` tried to bind port 4000 and failed with eaddrinuse in this environment.
frictions.resolution: Used file-level terminal validation only (cat + YAML parse) to keep verification within isolated, non-service boundary.

summary: Tested 3 terminal assertions: 2 passed (VAL-CLEAN-003, VAL-CLEAN-005) and 1 failed (VAL-CROSS-044: missing per-step status entries in pipeline_state.yaml).
